component.name: Boulder Relay
component.summary: GTK4 IRC client — any server, any channels

description.p: Boulder Relay is a GTK4 IRC client written in Rust using relm4. It provides a clean desktop interface for connecting to any IRC network (Libera, OFTC, etc.) with TLS, authentication, multi-channel chat, and live user lists.

No default channels are provided — you choose what to join. Settings are saved between sessions, chat includes timestamps and nick highlights, and slash commands (/join, /part, /clear, /msg, /me etc.) are supported. Ideal as a lightweight client or for custom distros.
Fix RHEL default channel to #rhel on Libera.Chat.
Window icon fix, background mode on close, and desktop notifications for IRC messages.
Persist custom channels, improve join parsing for any Libera channel, and add Fedora 44 COPR builds.
Add Fedora and RHEL default channels, grouped sidebar sections, and community color accents.
Fix white GNOME title bar by loading theme CSS after GTK init and replacing default window decorations.
Dark custom title bar, Gruvbox theme polish, nick highlights, /clear /part /help, and last channel restore.
Added persistent settings, disconnect control, timestamps, slash commands, and EL10 support.
